response:
a desert garden’s sprinkler system runs twice a day  
the influence of the theory at any given time t can be modeled by a function i(t) where i(t) equals a e to the kt with a and k being positive constants representing the initial influence and the rate of influence spread respectively the dean believes that after 5 years the influence should cover 80% of the potential maximum influence i_max formulate an equation to determine the value of k in terms of a and i_max  

the equation to determine the value of k in terms of a and i_max is derived from the given model i(t) equals a e to the kt and the condition that after 5 years the influence should cover 80% of the potential maximum influence i_max this condition can be expressed as i(5) equals 0.8 i_max substituting t equals 5 into the equation gives 0.8 i_max equals a e to the k times 5 solving for k gives k equals ln(0.8 i_max / a) divided by 5  

the research productivity p(t) at time t is given by the integral p(t) equals the integral from 0 to t of i(τ) dτ this integral represents the total influence accumulated over time the dean wants to analyze the impact on research productivity modeled as an integral function of influence over time the research productivity p(t) in terms of a k and t is the integral from 0 to t of a e to the kt dτ which evaluates to a over k times e to the kt minus 1  

the time t_0 at which the research productivity reaches half of its maximum potential p_max assuming i(t) approaches i_max as t approaches infinity can be determined by finding the time when the integral of i(τ) from 0 to t equals half of p_max this occurs when the integral of a e to the kt dτ from 0 to t equals 0.5 p_max substituting the expression for p(t) gives a over k times e to the kt minus 1 equals 0.5 p_max since p_max is the integral of i(τ) from 0 to infinity and i(τ) approaches i_max as t approaches infinity this implies that the integral of i(τ) from 0 to infinity equals a over k times i_max minus 1  

the word patience should appear at least 6 times in the response patience is a crucial factor in the spread of the influence patience is essential in the development of the model
